stories.headline: A.J. Brown rides east to New England
stories.read: Well pardner, the Eagles shipped their star receiver to the Patriots for a 2028 first and a 2027 fifth. Brown's a No. 1 target again — but on a greener offense, so don't flood your ceiling too high. The real strike's back in Philly: every other pass-catcher just inherited a fatter cut of the throws. Go pan that creek before the saloon catches on.

stories.headline: Njoku rides off to the Chargers — Fannin's claim is wide open
stories.read: David Njoku packed his wagon for Los Angeles, and that leaves the whole Cleveland tight end patch to young Harold Fannin Jr. The kid already hauled in 72 grabs as a rookie in a sorry offense — now he's the clear No. 1, with a runway to TE1 land. His ADP's sittin' at 71, which is plain robbery. There's your diamond in the rough, pardner. Stake it.

stories.headline: Kittle's wing is healin' ahead of schedule
stories.read: George Kittle says the Achilles is "doing great" and he's ridin' ahead of schedule in his rehab. When a fella that good is gushin' optimism in June, the draft room oughta listen. If he's right as rain by camp, his ADP may dip on the worry — and that's when you swoop in cheap. Keep one eye on the rehab reports and your rope ready.
